summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a brilliant 1968 show highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, an epic Eleven, and a powerful Other One.
review: Reviewers rave about the near‑perfect performance – especially the fiery Dark Star, the soaring Eleven (with standout Weir vocals), and the masterful Other One > Cryptical sequence – while noting only minor quirks like a splice before New Potato and a less‑interesting drum solo. The Charllie Miller soundboard source is praised for its high fidelity, making this one of the top 1968 releases.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are overwhelmingly positive (four 5‑star, one 4‑star) and focus on exceptional musical moments; recording quality is repeatedly called “good/excellent,” leading to a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Charllie Miller soundboard capture praised for clarity and overall fidelity; minor issue noted with the beginning of Dark Star being cut off and a possible splice before New Potato.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is considered the stronger half of the concert, with a powerful Dark Star and tight Eleven; second set holds high points (Other One > Cryptical) but suffers from an abrupt New Potato transition and a less engaging drum segment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is described as top‑notch, masterful on Dark Star and Death Don't Have No Mercy, feeding off the band.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass lines are highlighted as integral to the Eleven’s brilliance and overall group interplay.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocal contribution on the Eleven receives specific praise; reviewers note his part was nearly dismissed historically but shines here.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers are called fabulous early in the set, though the dedicated Drum solo is deemed less interesting.
